ELPAC Level 1
Lexile 1000–1300L
DTR | Listen to a Speaker Support an Opinion
LSO-MS7-L1-10001300-MSMATH7-DBT-CFI-02 | MS Math: Grade 7 | Probability Models (Simple and Compound Events) | Debate Duel | Cultural Fusion & Innovation
Where Six Meets Red
Click 🎧 to listen.
Listen to a conversation between two students. The boy is discussing his opinion about the grand prize rule for their festival game. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer four questions about the conversation and the evidence the boy gives to support his opinion. As you listen, you may take notes.
Listen to the Conversation
1QUESTION 1:
Why does the boy describe the small prize first?
2QUESTION 2:
How does the boy know that almost nobody wins the grand prize?
3QUESTION 3:
What does the boy say the chance of winning the grand prize is?
4QUESTION 4:
What does the boy think the festival should do about the grand prize?
Answer all 4 questions to submit.
Choose the best answer for each question. You may change your answers before submitting.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Identify why the boy brings up the small prize first. Identify how he found out how often the big prize is won. Identify the 1 out of 24 chance he gives the big prize. Identify what he thinks the festival should do.
CA ELD: PI.A.3, PI.B.5, PI.B.7, PI.B.8, PII.A.1 | CCSS: 7.SP.C.7, 7.SP.C.8 | MS Math: Grade 7
